title: Java Garbage Collection Eligibility
description: Trace when objects become eligible for garbage collection after references change.
learningGoals: Identify when an object becomes eligible for garbage collection.
Recall that System.gc() is only a request and does not guarantee collection.

completionChecks.prompt: When does the first Object become eligible for garbage collection?
completionChecks.explanation: first = null removes one reference, but second and cache[0] still reach the original object. After second points elsewhere and cache[0] is null, no live reference remains.

completionChecks.prompt: What happens when System.gc() is called here?
choices: The original object is guaranteed to be collected before the next line
The VM receives a request to run GC, but collection is not guaranteed
All objects allocated on the heap become unreachable
The code fails to compile because gc is private
completionChecks.explanation: The exam trap is confusing eligibility with collection. System.gc() may suggest a collection cycle, but Java does not promise the object is reclaimed immediately.

completionChecks.prompt: What happens if cache[0] = null is removed?
choices: The original object is still reachable through cache[0]
The original object becomes eligible after first = null
The array itself is collected immediately
The code fails because arrays cannot store Object references
completionChecks.explanation: second is reassigned, but the array element still points at the original object. A reachable array element is a live reference, so the object is not eligible yet.
